name: TradingDiary Pro
description_short: Desktop-based trading journal with institutional-grade analytics, 16+ broker integrations, and a one-time purchase model — no recurring subscription required.
description_long:
TradingDiary Pro is a powerful desktop trading journal built for serious traders who want deep performance analytics without a monthly subscription. Founded in 2009 and based in Hungary, it offers a one-time license at $149 that includes one year of updates and support, with an optional $49/year renewal thereafter — making it one of the most cost-effective long-term journaling solutions compared to cloud-based SaaS competitors.

The software connects directly to 16+ brokers including Interactive Brokers, thinkorswim, TD Ameritrade, Schwab, Fidelity, FXCM, MetaTrader 4, NinjaTrader, tastytrade, TradeStation, DEGIRO, Questrade, and others via one-click import. For unsupported brokers, universal CSV and Excel import ensures traders on any platform can capture their complete trade history with minimal friction.

Analytics go well beyond basic win/loss tracking. TradingDiary Pro calculates Sharpe Ratio, Sortino Ratio, R-Multiple distributions, Internal Rate of Return, Annual Return, Downside Deviation, and Standard Deviation. Version 24.1 introduced Market-to-Market cumulative reporting, showing daily gain/loss versus prior periods. Built-in capital gains tax reporting automatically generates taxable events in multiple formats, saving traders significant time at tax time.

Because TradingDiary Pro stores all trade data locally on your machine, traders retain full privacy and control — a meaningful differentiator from cloud-based journals. The customizable dashboard lets users configure which metrics and reports appear front-and-center, and multi-database support allows managing multiple accounts or strategies in separate portfolios without mixing data.

tiers.name: License
features: 1 year of updates and support included

tiers.name: Annual Support Renewal
features: Continued major and minor version updates

pros: One-time pricing eliminates ongoing subscription costs
Deep analytics including Sharpe/Sortino ratios, R-Multiple, and IRR
Local data storage ensures full privacy and offline access
16+ broker one-click import with CSV fallback for any platform
Built-in capital gains tax reporting saves time at tax time
cons: Desktop-only with no mobile app or web browser access
Annual fee required to continue receiving updates after first year
No AI-powered analysis or trade pattern recognition
Small independent team may mean slower feature development
best_for: active traders who want deep performance analytics
privacy-focused traders preferring local data storage
traders needing automated capital gains tax reports
meta_description: TradingDiary Pro: one-time $149 license, 16+ broker imports, Sharpe/Sortino analytics, and tax reporting. No subscription. 2026 honest review.
